prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene were computed using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (Bannwarth et al., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15, 1652–1671) via the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill. Calculation was run in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, on the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe). The N±1 states used cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were obtained by the Mulliken scheme within GFN2-xTB. Integrity checks passed: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000; trustworthy = true. No warnings were reported.

Headline result — Most electrophilic site: Fe (atom 11), f+ = 0.22885456. Most nucleophilic site: Fe (atom 11), f− = 0.25938191. Dual descriptor on Fe = −0.030527354 (negative → preferred nucleophilic-attack site). All 10 Cp carbons have f+ ≈ 0.013638, f− ≈ 0.012179; all 10 H atoms have f+ ≈ 0.063476, f− ≈ 0.061883. The dual descriptor is positive for all C and H atoms (~+0.0015–0.0016), classifying them as locally electrophilic relative to Fe. The near-perfect pentagonal symmetry of ferrocene is faithfully reflected in the numerical results.
